title: Wings of Light
wikitext:
[[Image:Death C0015 wings-of-light.jpg|thumb|right|Adam's white Wings of Light]]
[[Image:EoE Eva01 wings of light composite.jpg|thumb|left|Eva-01's yellow Wings of Light]]
The '''Wings of Light''' (光の翼, ''hikari no tsubasa''） appear in the TV Series and in the movies [[Death]] and [[End of Evangelion]]. They appear during both [[Second Impact]] and [[Third Impact]]. During Second Impact, [[Adam]] produces white Wings of Light and during Third Impact, [[Evangelion Unit-01]] produces white ones. While these are transparent, the wings that [[Rei Ayanami|Rei]]/[[Lilith]] produces during Third Impact are white and opaque, almost as if they were an extension of her physical body. 

==Symbolism==
The most obvious symbolism is that of the Christian [[Angels]] who are often depicted with wings.

==Analysis==
Although the true purpose of the Wings of Light are unknown, both occur during an event where souls are involved. Presumably during Second Impact, the souls of the [[Katsuragi Expedition]]'s team can be seen floating around when Adam spreads Wings of Light. During Third Impact, the souls of everyone on Earth are collected.



==In Rebuild of Evangelion==
{{Rebuildcontentstart}}
[[Image:Eva2-22 C0402D comp.jpg|thumb|left|Misato, her father, and the four Adams' wings.]]

Wings of light are possessed by the four [[Adams]] of Second Impact and are later developed by Eva-01 at [[Near Third Impact]] during its transformation into a luminous being. When Misato observes the latter, she comments on the similarity.
{{Rebuildcontentend}}
[[Category:A to Z]]
[[Category:Terminology]]
[[Category:Religious and Esoteric References]]

html:
The Wings of Light (光の翼, hikari no tsubasa） appear in the TV Series and in the movies Death and End of Evangelion. They appear during both Second Impact and Third Impact. During Second Impact, Adam produces white Wings of Light and during Third Impact, Evangelion Unit-01 produces white ones. While these are transparent, the wings that Rei/Lilith produces during Third Impact are white and opaque, almost as if they were an extension of her physical body.

Symbolism[edit]

The most obvious symbolism is that of the Christian Angels who are often depicted with wings.

Analysis[edit]

Although the true purpose of the Wings of Light are unknown, both occur during an event where souls are involved. Presumably during Second Impact, the souls of the Katsuragi Expedition's team can be seen floating around when Adam spreads Wings of Light. During Third Impact, the souls of everyone on Earth are collected.


In Rebuild of Evangelion[edit]

Wings of light are possessed by the four Adams of Second Impact and are later developed by Eva-01 at Near Third Impact during its transformation into a luminous being. When Misato observes the latter, she comments on the similarity.


This article bears the unpleasant designation of "Stub." This is a Bad Thing. Provide a great service to NGE fan-geeks everywhere by making it awesomer!

The Wings of Light (光の翼, hikari no tsubasa） appear in the TV Series and in the movies Death and End of Evangelion. They appear during both Second Impact and Third Impact. During Second Impact, Adam produces white Wings of Light and during Third Impact, Evangelion Unit-01 produces white ones. While these are transparent, the wings that Rei/Lilith produces during Third Impact are white and opaque, almost as if they were an extension of her physical body. 

Symbolism
The most obvious symbolism is that of the Christian Angels who are often depicted with wings.

Analysis
Although the true purpose of the Wings of Light are unknown, both occur during an event where souls are involved. Presumably during Second Impact, the souls of the Katsuragi Expedition's team can be seen floating around when Adam spreads Wings of Light. During Third Impact, the souls of everyone on Earth are collected.

In Rebuild of Evangelion

thumb|left|Misato, her father, and the four Adams' wings.

Wings of light are possessed by the four Adams of Second Impact and are later developed by Eva-01 at Near Third Impact during its transformation into a luminous being. When Misato observes the latter, she comments on the similarity.
